topIssues: The massive 'JOB' card and 'MORE EXAMPLES' section compete directly with the primary search input.
Excessive use of bordered boxes and pills creates visual clutter (Chrome Collapse violation).
The action buttons (Upload, Paste, Voice, Camera) are too prominent and distract from the text input.
Low contrast on the dark purple 'JOB' card makes it hard to read against the dark background.
The 'LENS' selectors are awkwardly placed to the right of the suggested prompts, breaking the visual flow.
recommendations: Remove the 'JOB' and 'MORE EXAMPLES' sections from the home screen to focus entirely on the search input.
Remove borders from the suggested prompts and action buttons, using subtle background colors or typography for hierarchy instead.
Move the 'LENS' selectors to a more integrated position, perhaps as a dropdown within the search bar itself.
Simplify the action buttons (Upload, Paste, etc.) into a single 'attachment' icon inside the search bar.
Increase the contrast of secondary text and card backgrounds to improve readability in dark mode.

dominantJob.reasoning: Because the screen displays numerous empty state containers and post-generation actions before the user has even started a session, which violates the principle of having one clear primary action — compared to ChatGPT's focused empty state — this scores 4/10.

topIssues: Pre-rendered 'PENDING' boxes clutter the empty state and distract from the primary input.
Post-generation actions ('Save report', 'Share link') are visible before any content exists.
Excessive use of borders on almost every UI element creates visual noise.
Conflicting copy between the header ('Start a live session') and input placeholder ('Continue the live session...').
The primary input field is pushed down the page by empty containers.
recommendations: Remove the four 'PENDING' boxes from the initial empty state; reveal them progressively as the AI generates the response.
Hide the bottom action buttons ('Save report', etc.) until a response has been fully generated.
Remove borders from the suggestion and persona pills, using subtle background fills instead to reduce chrome.
Update the input placeholder to match the initial state (e.g., 'Ask a question to start...').
Center the input field vertically in the empty state, similar to ChatGPT, to focus the user on the primary action.

topIssues: Three-column layout creates competing primary actions (search vs. browse vs. read).

recommendations: Collapse the three-column layout: If a report is open, it should take over the main central area, pushing navigation/search to a collapsible sidebar.

Simplify the central 'Starter' cards to just title, description, and a single badge, removing the nested grid of metadata.

Add inline, clickable source citations to the report content to build trust and verify AI reasoning.

topIssues: Extreme 'chrome' overload: Every element is in a bordered box, creating massive visual clutter.
Lack of a dominant job: The screen presents ~15 distinct actions with no clear primary path.
Visual fragmentation: The three-column layout with varying box heights creates a disorganized feel.
Competing CTAs: 'Start in Chat' and 'Open Reports' are placed at the bottom of a column rather than in a prominent, guiding position.
Manual burden: The UI suggests the user must manually curate a lot of context rather than the system learning it automatically.
recommendations: Remove all borders from list items and informational sections; use whitespace and typography for hierarchy.
Consolidate the 'Add...' actions into a single primary 'Add Context' button or a unified input area.
Move the 'Start in Chat' CTA to a more prominent, fixed position (e.g., top right or a sticky bottom bar) as the primary exit point.
Redesign the stats (Files Ready, etc.) as simple large numbers with labels, without enclosing them in cards.
Group the 'Context Already Here' and 'Recent File Groups' into a single, cleaner list or table format without individual borders.
